name: Literature Systems Map
summary: A starter lens for seeing literature as an interacting system rather than a list of isolated facts.
whenToUseIt: Use it when you need to orient quickly, compare subdomains, or explain how literature connects to the wider academy.
steps: Define the core purpose of literature
List the main inputs, constraints, and outputs
Map the adjacent subjects or institutions involved
Identify the leverage points and failure points
example: Create a one-page systems map showing how literature appears in the real world and what it interacts with.
